behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the segmenter’s local parsing logic for newline-delimited statements. A direct parser test with no filesystem/network/subprocess catches the regression. Higher levels were considered but rejected: behavior text is only about segmentation, not cross-module handoff or real shell execution.
behaviors.test_description: Call the command-segmentation function with a multi-line shell string (assignments + command) and assert it returns multiple ordered segments instead of one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundary: segmentation output must feed trigger classification correctly. Unit tests of classifier-only or segmenter-only can miss the handoff bug that produced `not_a_trigger`. System rejected: no real subprocess/network required to detect this classification path.
behaviors.test_description: Run the hook’s command-analysis pipeline end-to-end in-process (segmenter + trigger detector) on a multi-line block ending in `gh pr create`; assert trigger type is PR-create, not `not_a_trigger`.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is the same cross-module boundary as behavior 2, but for false-positive prevention. Need parser/classifier wiring with realistic multi-line input. System rejected because no real `gh` process invocation is required; classification is deterministic text analysis.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process command-analysis pipeline on multi-line assignments plus `gh issue create`; assert result is not PR-create trigger and falls into expected non-trigger/other-trigger category.

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary spans detection logic, PR identifier reconstruction, and persistence adapter (local file/repo state). This is multi-module with local filesystem, which fits Integration. System rejected: behavior does not require real network/subprocess; deterministic fixture payloads can drive identifier reconstruction.
behaviors.test_description: Using a temp workspace, feed a detected PR-create event (with representative command/output metadata) through hook handling path and assert persisted state contains the expected normalized PR identifier.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Critical failure is contract compatibility between two local modules (PostToolUse writer and PreToolUse reader/enforcer) via local state file format. Integration is minimum to catch schema/field mismatch. System rejected: no OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond local file I/O is required. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no LLM-dependent step.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp directory, invoke post-tool path to write review-gate state after PR-create detection, then invoke pre-tool enforcement hook against that state; assert it loads successfully and enforces gate policy.
